evals.prompt: Iowa S-corp consulting practice (one shareholder-officer). Form 1120-S 2024: gross receipts $480K (Line 1a), ordinary business income $310K (Line 21). Schedule K Box 1: $310K. Officer compensation (Form 1125-E): $30,000. Distributions (Schedule M-2 Column (a) decrease): $260,000. AAA beginning $40K, ending $90K. No C-corp E&P.
evals.expected_output: Summarizes 2024 1120-S: gross receipts $480K, ordinary income $310K, officer W-2 $30K, distributions $260K, ending AAA $90K. Flags TWO red flags routed: (1) Watson reasonable-comp pattern — W-2 of $30K is 9.7% of $310K ordinary income, well below industry comparables for an Iowa CPA / consultant; route to `predict-reasonable-comp`. (2) Distributions exceed AAA + basis if shareholder's stock basis was less than $40K beginning; this would trigger §1368(b) capital gain or §1368(c) E&P-step (no E&P here, so basis-step). Need shareholder's stock-basis schedule to fully assess. NO tax-position advice; routes to `predict-reasonable-comp` and `tax-research-entity` (subchapter-s).
expectations: Extracts and reports core figures (gross receipts, ordinary income, officer W-2, distributions, AAA)
Flags need for shareholder stock-basis schedule

evals.prompt: Multi-member LLC taxed as partnership. Form 1065 2024: gross receipts $2.1M, ordinary business income $640K. Two equal partners, A and B. A contributed appreciated real estate (FMV $800K, basis $400K) at formation 2 years ago. Schedule B Question 12 (§704(b) special allocations) marked 'No'. Schedule K-1 Part II shows tax-basis capital accounts; no §704(c) layer indicated. Schedule M-2 ending capital reconciles to Schedule L equity.
evals.expected_output: Summarizes 2024 1065: gross receipts $2.1M, ordinary income $640K, two equal partners, A and B. Flags: (1) §704(c) built-in gain on A's contributed real estate ($400K) is NOT visibly tracked — Schedule K-1 Part II should show §704(c) layer per §1.704-3 method (traditional, traditional-with-curative, or remedial); the absence is a documentation red flag. (2) §704(c)(1)(B) and §737 7-year disguised-sale window started at formation; 2 years in. (3) §704(b) Question 12 marked No suggests no special allocations, but §704(c) is mandatory regardless of §704(b) elections. Route to `tax-research-entity` (subchapter-k). Flag for partnership-engagement review.
expectations: Identifies §704(c) tracking gap on contributed appreciated property

evals.prompt: Form 1120 2024: gross receipts $5.2M, total income $5.5M, NOL deduction $200K (Line 29a), taxable income $50K, total tax $10,500. Schedule G Q3 indicates new 60% shareholder (acquired in March 2024). Prior-year NOL carryforward at beginning of 2024 was $1.4M. Schedule J shows §382 limitation calculation referenced.
evals.expected_output: Summarizes 2024 1120: gross receipts $5.2M, total income $5.5M, NOL utilized $200K, TI $50K, total tax $10,500 (effective ~ 21% on TI). Flag: §382 ownership change indicated — 60% shareholder acquired in March 2024 likely triggered an ownership change (50% over 3-year testing period). The $200K NOL utilized may be subject to §382 limitation; the $1.4M remaining carryforward is similarly subject. Schedule J reference confirms practitioner aware. Route to `tax-research-entity` for ownership-change-date determination, §382 limit computation, and Reg. §1.382-3 ownership-change-detection rules. Flag NOL-carryforward-tracking schedule maintenance.
expectations: Extracts core figures including NOL utilization
